## 10. Reproducibility Appendix

| Release-summary reuse | Treat `release_summary_draft.md` as stable enough to seed future README-adjacent short updates, while keeping the manuscript bundle as the authoritative source. | The release summary now inherits the frozen scope, the mixed `R2` wording, and the `D0` endpoint boundary without introducing new claims or marketing-style compression. Using it as the short-update source reduces future public-surface churn. | A later manuscript or public-surface change introduces materially new quantitative detail or shifts a claim boundary that the release summary no longer captures cleanly. | `docs/publication_record/release_summary_draft.md`, `docs/publication_record/manuscript_bundle_draft.md`, `README.md`, `STATUS.md` |

| Post-`P7` next phase | Treat the freeze-candidate checkpoint as the fixed baseline and move the next paper-facing work into `P8` bundle lock, `H2` audit promotion, and `P9` restrained release sync. | The next risk is package and governance drift, not another freeze pass or a structure rewrite. | A later audit or review requirement proves one missing evidence class that cannot be resolved inside the current frozen bundle. | `docs/milestones/P7_manuscript_freeze_candidate_and_release_preflight/result_digest.md`, `docs/publication_record/paper_package_plan.md`, `docs/plans/2026-03-19-post-p7-submission-release-stabilization-master-plan.md` |

| Evidence reopen discipline | Reopen evidence only through one named `E1` patch lane, and always return control to the locked paper/release lanes. | This keeps unattended runs from silently widening precision, systems, or compiled scope by wording drift. | A later scope decision intentionally replaces the current frozen endpoint with a new evidence program. | `docs/publication_record/conditional_reopen_protocol.md`, `docs/milestones/E1_conditional_evidence_reopen_protocol/result_digest.md` |
`P8` closed the submission-candidate bundle-lock pass on the frozen paper
